channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fatima Sana appointed Pakistan Women’s Captain for T20 World Cup and Ireland Tri-Series
item.description: Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has announced its 15-member women’s squad for the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup 2026 and the upcoming tri-series in Ireland. Fatima Sana, aged 24, will lead the team for the s...

item.title: AFL D-PIC saga continues as court orders release of documents
item.description: The latest developments in the AFL D-PIC saga have taken a significant turn with the ordering of documents, shedding light on ongoing investigations and legal proceedings. This move is expected to pro...

item.title: Kenya Fuel Prices Rise as EPRA Announces New Rates Compared to East African Neighbours
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) in Kenya has announced new fuel prices effective after Thursday, 14 May 2026. This comes after international oil prices went up due to the conflict...

item.title: Mark McCloskey returns to represent Jan. 6 defendants following medical recovery and potential compensation fund
item.description: Mark McCloskey, the St. Louis attorney known for brandishing a gun at Black Lives Matter protesters in 2020, has informed his Jan. 6 clients that he is resuming their legal representation after previo...

item.title: Understanding Energy Gels and Their Use in Endurance Running
item.description: Energy gels have become a common fuel source for marathon runners and other endurance athletes, with over 70% reportedly using them during races. These gels provide a concentrated form of carbohydrate...

item.title: Matatiele Residents Demand Checks on Undocumented Shops; Legal Immigrant Businesses Urged Not to Close
item.description: In Matatiele, Eastern Cape, immigrant shop owners closed their businesses on Thursday as around 60 residents took part in a march organised by the Cleaning Eastern Cape Together Movement. The proteste...

item.title: Karachi’s Paver Experiment: Can Blocks Solve Waterlogging and Road Damage?
item.description: Karachi has been experimenting with paver blocks on roads in areas prone to waterlogging and sewerage issues. The city administration, led by local chairmen and Mayor Murtaza Wahab, has invested heavi...

item.title: 19 Women Photographers Making an Impact in Contemporary Photography
item.description: In celebration of Women’s History Month 2026, LensCulture highlights nineteen women photographers whose work and vision are making a significant mark on contemporary photography. The article showcases...

item.title: Person airlifted to hospital following serious head-on collision north of Edmonton
item.description: Emergency crews responded to a serious head-on collision north of Edmonton that left one व्यक्ति with significant injuries requiring air transport to hospital. The crash, which involved two vehicles t...

item.title: Senate did not accept subpoena for CCTV footage of Senate shooting, says Ombudsman
item.description: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla reported that the Senate refused to accept the subpoena issued for CCTV footage related to the shooting incident that happened on May 13. According to Remulla, the proc...

item.title: Kizz Daniel to Drop Two Albums This Year Following Previous EP
item.description: Afrobeats star Kizz Daniel don announce say e dey plan to release two albums for 2026. After e release 'Uncle K' EP for 2025, the singer dey ready to make am up by dropping two full projects this year...

item.title: Trump-China Summit Ends With Limited Achievements and Focus on Long-Term Relations
item.description: US President Donald Trump completed a two-day visit to China without achieving major trade or strategic wins, despite giving plenty praise to Chinese President Xi Jinping. The visit, first by a US pre...

item.title: Delhi Fire Services Urges Government to Recruit 6,600+ Staff Amid Severe Shortage
item.description: The Delhi Fire Services (DFS) has formally requested the Delhi government to urgently increase its workforce, citing a critical shortage of over 6,600 personnel. Presently, the department operates wit...

item.title: Marco Rubio uses alternate Chinese name amid past travel ban
item.description: During U.S. President Donald Trump's state visit to Beijing, attention was drawn to Marco Rubio, the U.S. Secretary of State and former senator, who was previously banned from China in 2020. Observers...

item.title: Frank Luntz criticizes Trump for floating Vance-Rubio 2028 ticket rivalry during midterms
item.description: On Friday, CNN's Brianna Keilar interviewed longtime Republican pollster and focus group expert Frank Luntz about a recent clip of President Donald Trump. In the clip, Trump publicly discussed the pos...

item.title: Sindh removes fixed market closing times to support businesses and citizens
item.description: Sindh Senior Minister Sharjeel Memon announced on Saturday that the provincial government has officially lifted the 9pm closing restriction for shops, markets, malls, hotels, restaurants, food outlets...

item.title: Former RAW Chief Admits Pakistan Remains Diplomatic Force Despite Tensions with India
item.description: Former RAW chief AS Dulat has admitted that India’s long-standing attempts to isolate Pakistan at the international level have not succeeded. Speaking to a British news channel, Dulat said India repea...

item.title: Luzon Grid Back to Normal Operations After Series of Red and Yellow Alerts
item.description: The Luzon grid has returned to normal conditions as of Saturday morning, May 16, 2026, following several days of red and yellow alerts that caused rotating brownouts in parts of Luzon and Visayas. Acc...

item.title: Officials Say Gordie Howe Bridge Opening Depends on Testing Amid U.S.-Canada Trade Talks
item.description: The opening of the $6.4 billion Gordie Howe International Bridge, connecting Windsor, Ontario, to Detroit, Michigan, remains delayed, with Canadian officials citing necessary testing and commissioning...

item.title: Hawks Officer Testifies on Messages and Threats Related to R286 Million Cocaine Seizure
item.description: A Hawks officer, Lieutenant Colonel Nkoana Joseph Sebola, has revealed messages allegedly exchanged by individuals connected to a massive cocaine consignment worth R286 million that was intercepted in...
